At the end of $2000$, Walter was one third as old as his grandmother. The sum of the years in which they were born was $3896$. How old will Walter be at the end of $2005$?
- **(A)** 30
- **(B)** 31
- **(C)** 32
- **(D)** 33
- **(E)** 34

In rectangle $ABCD$, $\overline{AB}=24$ and $\overline{BC}=7$. Let $E$ be a point on $\overline{CD}$ such that $\angle CBE=30^\circ$. What is $\overline{AE}$?
$\textbf{(A)}\ \dfrac{13\sqrt{3}}{3}\qquad\textbf{(B)}\ 14\qquad\textbf{(C)}\ \dfrac{14\sqrt{3}}{3}\qquad\textbf{(D)}\ 15\qquad\textbf{(E)}\ \dfrac{15\sqrt{3}}{3}$

In a monthly calendar, assume the date behind letter D is $d+5$. The date behind E is two days after D and the date behind F is $d+20$. If the sum of the dates behind D and F is equal to the sum of the dates behind E and another unspecified date behind letter X, determine the letter X from the following options:

$$\text{(A)}\ \text{G} \qquad \text{(B)}\ \text{H} \qquad \text{(C)}\ \text{I} \qquad \text{(D)}\ \text{J} \qquad \text{(E)}\ \text{K}$$
$$\text{(A)}\ \text{G}\ (d+10) \qquad \text{(B)}\ \text{H}\ (d+12) \qquad \text{(C)}\ \text{I}\ (d+15) \qquad \text{(D)}\ \text{J}\ (d+17) \qquad \text{(E)}\ \text{K}\ (d+18)$$

A polygon is formed by removing a triangle from one corner of a rectangle. The rectangle has dimensions 10 units by 6 units, and the triangle removed has a base along one of the longer sides of the rectangle and a height of 4 units. Determine the perimeter of the resulting polygon.
(A) 22 + 2sqrt(25)
(B) 22 + 2sqrt(29)
(C) 22 + 2sqrt(34)
(D) 22 + 10

Tetrahedron $PQRS$ has $PQ=6$, $PR=4$, $PS=5$, $QR=5$, $QS=7$, and $RS=8$. Calculate the volume of the tetrahedron.
$\textbf{(A) } 36\qquad\textbf{(B) } 18\qquad\textbf{(C) } 24\qquad\textbf{(D) } 30\qquad\textbf{(E) } 12$

Solve the equation $\sqrt{x + 16} - \frac{8}{\sqrt{x + 16}} = 3$. Determine if there are extraneous roots, and if the roots are between certain numerical ranges:
$\textbf{(A)}\ \text{an extraneous root between } -8\text{ and } -4 \\
\textbf{(B)}\ \text{an extraneous root between }-16\text{ and }-12\\
\textbf{(C)}\ \text{a true root between }30\text{ and }35\qquad
\textbf{(D)}\ \text{two true roots}\\
\textbf{(E)}\ \text{two extraneous roots}$
$\textbf{(A)}\ \text{an extraneous root between } -8\text{ and } -4 \\
\textbf{(B)}\ \text{an extraneous root between }-16\text{ and }-12\\
\textbf{(C)}\ \text{a true root between }30\text{ and }35\qquad
\textbf{(D)}\ \text{two true roots}\\
\textbf{(E)}\ \text{two extraneous roots}$
ch:
\text{two extraneous roots}$
$\textbf{(A)}\ \text{an extraneous root between } -8\text{ and } -4 \\
\textbf{(B)}\ \text{an extraneous root between }-16\text{ and }-12\\
\textbf{(C)}\ \text{a true root between }30\text{ and }35\qquad
\textbf{(D)}\ \text{two true roots}\\
\textbf{(E)}\ \text{two extraneous roots}

p:
Paul owes Paula $45$ cents and has a pocket full of $10$-cent coins, $20$-cent coins, and $50$-cent coins. What is the difference between the largest and the smallest number of coins he can use to pay Paula exactly $45$ cents?
$\textbf{(A) }0\qquad\textbf{(B) }1\qquad\textbf{(C) }2\qquad\textbf{(D) }3\qquad \textbf{(E) }4$

Bricklayer Brenda would take $8$ hours to build a chimney alone, and bricklayer Brandon would take $12$ hours to build it alone. When they work together, their combined output is decreased by $12$ bricks per hour. Working together, they build the chimney in $6$ hours. How many bricks are in the chimney?
$\textbf{(A)}\ 200 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 250 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 288 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 300 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 350$

In a family chess tournament, a father, his sister, his son, and his daughter are participants. The worst and the best players are twins, and the best player is older than the worst player by exactly one year. The worst and the best players are not of the same sex. Who is the worst player?
$\textbf{(A)}\ \text{the father} \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ \text{his son} \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ \text{his sister} \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ \text{his daughter}$
$\textbf{(A)}\ \text{the father} \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ \text{his son} \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ \text{his sister} \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ \text{his daughter}$

The digits of Helen's age are the reverse of Ellen's age. In ten years, Helen will be three times as old as Ellen will be then. Determine the difference in their current ages.
$\mathrm{(A) \ } 54 \qquad \mathrm{(B) \ } 45 \qquad \mathrm{(C) \ } 36 \qquad \mathrm{(D) \ } 27 \qquad \mathrm{(E) \ } 18$

Given the true statement: If a polygon is a rhombus, then it is a parallelogram. Determine which of the converse and the inverse of this statement is:
- $\textbf{(A)}\ \text{only the converse is true}$
- $\textbf{(B)}\ \text{only the inverse is true}$
- $\textbf{(C)}\ \text{both are true}$
- $\textbf{(D)}\ \text{neither is true}$
- $\textbf{(E)}\ \text{the inverse is true, but the converse is sometimes true}$
- $\textbf{(A)}\ \text{only the converse is true}$
- $\textbf{(B)}\ \text{only the inverse is true}$
- $\textbf{(C)}\ \text{both are true}$
- $\textbf{(D)}\ \text{neither is true}$
- $\textbf{(E)}\ \text{the inverse is true, but the converse is sometimes true}$
ch:
\text{the inverse is true, but the converse is sometimes true}$
- $\textbf{(A)}\ \text{only the converse is true}$
- $\textbf{(B)}\ \text{only the inverse is true}$
- $\textbf{(C)}\ \text{both are true}$
- $\textbf{(D)}\ \text{neither is true}$
- $\textbf{(E)}\ \text{the inverse is true, but the converse is sometimes true}

Consider the product $(2 + x^2)(3 - x^3 + x^5)$. What is this expression when fully expanded?
A) $6 + 3x^2 - 2x^3 + x^5$  
B) $6 + 3x^2 - 3x^3 + x^5 + x^7$  
C) $6 + 3x^2 - 2x^3 + x^5 + x^7$  
D) $5 + 2x^2 - 2x^3 + x^5$  
E) $4 + x^2 - x^3 + x^5 + x^7$

How many of the base-ten numerals for the positive integers less than or equal to $2500$ contain the digit $0$?
$\textbf{(A)}\ 942\qquad\textbf{(B)}\ 952\qquad\textbf{(C)}\ 962\qquad\textbf{(D)}\ 972\qquad\textbf{(E)}\ 982$

Two positive numbers have a product of 12. The reciprocal of the first number is 3 times the reciprocal of the other number. What is the sum of these two numbers?
$\textbf{(A)}\ 10\qquad\textbf{(B)}\ 7\qquad\textbf{(C)}\ 8\qquad\textbf{(D)}\ 9\qquad\textbf{(E)}\ 5$

A regular hexagon and a circle share the same center. The circle has a radius of $\sqrt{3}$, while each side of the hexagon is $2$. Calculate the area inside the circle but outside the hexagon.
$\textbf{(A)}\ 3\pi - 4\sqrt{3} \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 3\pi - 6\sqrt{3} \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 3\pi - 8\sqrt{3} \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 3\pi - 10\sqrt{3}$

What is the least possible value of $(xy-1)^3 + (x+y)^3$ for real numbers $x$ and $y$?
A) -1
B) 0
C) 1
D) 2
